questions.question: What is hashing? Explain the components of a hash table with a suitable example.
keyPoints: Hashing is a technique that maps data of variable size to fixed-size values using a hash function
Three components: array (bucket table), hash function, collision resolution mechanism
Hash code is the output of the hash function; index = hash code % table size
Example: inserting keys into a table using h(k) = k mod m
Discuss advantages: O(1) average search, insert, delete

questions.question: Define load factor. Explain why it is important and how it affects the performance of a hash table.
keyPoints: Load factor alpha = n/m where n = number of elements, m = table size
Higher load factor leads to more collisions and slower operations
Typically keep alpha < 0.7 for efficient O(1) operations
When threshold is exceeded, the table should be resized (rehashing)

questions.question: Compare the time complexity of search operations in arrays, linked lists, balanced BSTs, and hash tables.
keyPoints: Balanced BST: O(log n) binary comparison
Hash Table: O(1) average case, O(n) worst case when all keys collide
